zh.lifeSummary: 當John Bourchier Sayer於1528年11月出生在荷蘭霍蘭阿姆斯特爾達姆時，他的父親Richard Sayer20歲，母親LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT19歲。他於1548年與Elizabeth Hawkins在英國結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1595年11月12日在荷蘭霍蘭阿姆斯特爾達姆享年67歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Bourchier Sayer于1528年11月出生在荷兰霍蘭阿姆斯特爾達姆时，他的父亲Richard Sayer20岁，母亲LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT19岁。他于1548年与Elizabeth Hawkins在英国结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1595年11月12日在荷兰霍蘭阿姆斯特爾達姆享年67岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

nameMeanings.name: Sayer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Sayre
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Sayer  .

variants.name: Sears
meanings: Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair ‘son of Saoghar’, a borrowing in Anglo-Norman Ireland of the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (Old French Seheri), from ancient Germanic Sigeheri, Sighari, composed of the elements sig‘victory’ + hari, heri ‘army’. See Sayer 5.
English: variant of   Sayers  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian) Cyr and French Saint-Cyr (see St. Cyr ), with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Sear
meanings: English: variant of   Sayer  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian)   Cyr  .

variants.name: Searson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5) + -son.

English: variant of Searston, a habitational name from Syerston (Nottinghamshire). The placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Sighere (genitive Sigheres) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Sears  .

variants.name: Sare
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Sayer  .
English: variant of   Sara  .
Croatian (Šare): nickname derived from šar(en) ‘mottled, gaily colored’, possibly applied to someone with hair of different colors. Compare Share .

variants.name: Saier
meanings: South German: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of Middle High German saien ‘to sow’. Compare Sayer 8.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sayers
meanings: English: variant of Sayer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name is also established in Ireland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair; see Sears .

variants.name: Sawyer
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who earned his living by sawing wood, from Middle English sauer(e), sauw(i)er, also sagh(i)er, sag(i)er ‘sawyer’, a derivative of Old English sagu ‘saw’.

Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname, or translation into English of Jewish Seger or some other surname meaning ‘sawyer’, e.g. German Sager and Slovenian Žagar (see Zagar ).

variants.name: Seekings
meanings: English: from the unrecorded Middle English personal name Saykin, a pet form of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5). The final -s is post-medieval and excrescent.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin an occupational name for either a scribe or binder of books (a derivative of Middle English boke ‘book’, Old English bōc), or else a bleacher of cloth (a derivative of Middle English bouken ‘to steep in lye’, Middle Dutch būken).

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: from an Old Norse personal name based on the name of the god Þórr (Thor) + geirr ‘spear’.

historicEvents.description: William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.
The siege of Leiden between the rebels and the Spanish. 1574 The dikes were opened allowing a Dutch fleet to sail over the flood waters and rescue Leiden.
The Dutch declared their independence from Spain.

historicEvents.title: The Golden Age
historicEvents.description: "The 17th century era, from roughly 1585 to 1672, was considered to be Amsterdam's ""Golden Age"". The city had formed a worldwide trading network with countries like Africa, Indonesia, Brazil, and North America. The city grew significantly during this time, and was regarded as the wealthiest city in the western world."

en.lifeSummary: When John Bourchier Sayer was born in November 1528, in Amsterdam, Holland, Netherlands, his father, Richard Sayer, was 20 and his mother, LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT, was 19. He married Elizabeth Hawkins in 1548, in England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 12 November 1595, in Amsterdam, Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 67, and was buried in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Bourchier Sayer est né en novembre 1528, à Amsterdam, Holland, Pays-Bas, son père, Richard Sayer, avait 20 ans et sa mère, LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Hawkins en 1548, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 12 novembre 1595, à Amsterdam, Holland, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Bourchier Sayer im November 1528 in Amsterdam, Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Sayer 20 und seine Mutter LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT 19. Er hat 1548 in England Elizabeth Hawkins geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 12. November 1595 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Amsterdam, Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Bourchier Sayer è nato il nel novembre 1528 nel Amsterdam, Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Richard Sayer, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Hawkins nel 1548, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 12 novembre 1595, nel Amsterdam, Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: John Bourchier Sayerが1528年11月にオランダホーランドアムステルダムで生まれた時，父Richard Sayerは20歳，母LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETTは19歳であった。1548年にイギリスでElizabeth Hawkinsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1595年11月12日にオランダホーランドアムステルダムで67歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドアムステルダムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Bourchier Sayer(이)가 1528년 11월 네덜란드 홀랜드 아스터다에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Sayer(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1548년 영국에서 Elizabeth Hawkins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1595년 11월 12일 네덜란드 홀랜드 아스터다에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Bourchier Sayer nasceu em novembro de 1528, em Amsterdam, Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Richard Sayer, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Hawkins em 1548, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de novembro de 1595, em Amsterdam, Holland, Países Baixos, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Bourchier Sayer родился ноябрь 1528 г. в местности амстирдам, холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Richard Sayer было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Hawkins в 1548 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 12 ноября 1595 г. в местности амстирдам, холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Bourchier Sayer nació en noviembre de 1528, en Amsterdam, Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Richard Sayer, tenía 20 años y su mamá, LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Hawkins en 1548, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 12 de noviembre de 1595, en Amsterdam, Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos.
